mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier discounting and higher risk appetite into equities, so 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive when the policy rate trend is down over the prior quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not above baseline in periods where FEDFUNDS has declined for at least 3 consecutive months, or if the conditional hit rate falls below 55% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a prior-month threshold signals labor-market deterioration and slowing household income growth, which should push 20-day S&P 500 returns negative as cyclical earnings expectations reset lower.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points from the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in a majority of months immediately following the trigger, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional average by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST and RSAFS both weaken at the same time, housing activity and retail spending are confirming a demand slowdown, so S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days turn negative as revenue expectations fall across cyclicals.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value was 1588.0 in 2006-11 and RSAFS vintage value was 369865.0 in 2006-12
failure_condition: Reject if the joint downside condition on HOUST and RSAFS does not predict lower 60-day forward returns than either series alone, or if the interaction term is not statistically stronger than both univariate effects in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A downward move in INDPRO indicates weakening industrial output and slower real activity, which should reduce earnings momentum and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day SPX forward returns are not negative on average after monthly declines in INDPRO, or if the signal loses sign consistency across two consecutive business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level above the neutral zone compresses valuation multiples and raises recession concern; when policy is still restrictive, S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are negative.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS above 5.0% do not show lower 20-day forward SPX returns than periods below 5.0%, or if triggered returns are not negative in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies broad final-demand weakness; when both are falling, the market should reprice earnings lower and S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days should be negative.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage value was 11443.5 in 2006-07 and RSAFS vintage value was 369865.0 in 2006-12
failure_condition: Reject if the joint decline in GDPC1 and RSAFS does not improve explanatory power versus either series alone, or if 60-day SPX returns are not negative in the majority of joint-decline episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a low and stable UNRATE supports earnings and sentiment, so the market keeps trending higher and S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive unless unemployment begins to rise sharply.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains at or below 4.7% and PAYEMS is flat-to-rising over 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 4.5% and PAYEMS was 136214.0 in 2006-12
failure_condition: Reject if low-unemployment episodes do not correspond to above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if a rising unemployment sub-regime produces the same positive sign as the stable-labor sub-regime.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO falls while CPIAUCSL keeps rising, the economy is experiencing stagflation pressure: weaker output and sticky inflation raise margin and discount-rate stress, so S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days turn negative.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value was 201.7 in 2006-11 and INDPRO vintage value was 112.3227 in 2006-11
failure_condition: Reject if the combined falling-INDPRO/rising-CPI pattern does not predict lower 5-day SPX returns than either proxy alone, or if the sign flips positive in repeated inflationary slowdown episodes.
